name: Vancouver (Canada)

colors.symbolism: the Pacific Ocean and the natural harbour
the waters of Vancouver's port and ocean position
the land on which the city is built and its surrounding forests
the gold of the city's badge — the mural crown, axe and paddle

description: A white field with five wavy blue horizontal bars and a green pentagon at the hoist; the green pentagon bears a gold city badge consisting of a mural crown surmounting a crossed axe and paddle.
history: Designed by Robert Watt — at the time director of the Museum of Vancouver and later Chief Herald of Canada — and adopted by Vancouver City Council on 17 May 1983.

features.symbolism: five wavy blue bars on a white field representing the Pacific waters of Vancouver's natural harbour and English Bay
a green five-sided wedge at the hoist representing the land on which the city is built and the surrounding forests of the Pacific Northwest

features.role: city badge — mural crown above crossed axe and paddle
features.symbolism: the city badge in gold: the mural crown reflects Vancouver's status as an incorporated city, while the crossed axe and paddle stand for its traditional industries of logging and fishing
